page: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running on the x86_64 architecture. This server features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and is bundled with 2458 GB of local storage. It utilizes the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64lds_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, scoring 62160.81 in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and 16454.0 in Geekbench multi-core. Memory performance is highly efficient, with large-block uncached read bandwidth reaching 290.71 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 133.7 GB/sec. The processor's instruction set extensions, including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, contribute to strong scores in Passmark CPU encryption and software compilation. Single-core performance and LLM inference tasks fall within the average range.

The server offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, maximizing CPU resource density relative to memory allocation. However, with only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, memory-bound applications may face resource constraints. The inclusion of 2458 GB of local storage and strong database performance, as evidenced by 69.1 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, makes this instance highly suitable for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, database operations, and parallelized batch processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and comes bundled with 2458 GB of local storage. The server delivers strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by its stress-ng multi-core score of 62160.81 and Passmark CPU score of 61072.79. It also exhibits high memory bandwidth and exceptional database throughput, achieving 69.1 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. While single-core performance and LLM inference speeds are average, its high compute density and local storage capacity make it highly efficient for parallel processing, software compilation, static web serving, and database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v5 is a compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v5 features 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four vCPUs, fast local storage, and strong multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory
2458 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU and high-throughput memory bandwidth performance
Excellent database execution, achieving 69.1 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and parallel batch processing
